extraction_notes: Fields 'definition', 'family', 'name', 'primary_room_type_codes', 'key_cues_2d', and 'tier' extracted directly from Activity_Affordances sheet.
Fields 'primary_room_types' (human-readable) and 'room_signatures' extracted by joining Activity_Affordances with Room_Typology sheet on RoomType_ID.

Fields 'signature_artefacts' and 'key_spatial_relations' derived from Room_Typology.Signature_Artifacts and Room_Typology.Signature_Layout_Cues for primary rooms, cross-referenced with Activity_Affordances.Key_Cues_2D.

Fields 'positive_indicators' and 'negative_indicators' inferred from Key_Cues_2D text and Activity_Indicators sheet. The taxonomy does not have explicit positive/negative indicator lists; these are extracted and structured from the cue descriptions.
Field 'measurement_items' added from Activity_Indicators sheet — includes all Rating_Item and Behavioral_Proxy entries for each affordance.
Field 'coco_proxy_classes' added as cross-reference to coco_to_taxonomy_map.json for downstream segmentation pipeline use.
Field 'vlm_scoring_guidance' inferred from Key_Cues_2D field and taxonomy methodology notes.
Fields 'inferability_2d' and 'vr_enrichment' extracted from Room_Typology sheet for primary rooms.

L059.name: Sleep (Primary)
L059.definition: Suitability of the pictured space for doing sleep (primary) (given typical user intentions).
key_spatial_relations: enclosed private room with door
soft textile surfaces dominate
low ambient task lighting or dimmable fixtures
minimal through-traffic / low disturbance cues
circulation space around bed
positive_indicators: Nightstand or bedside table adjacent to bed
Curtains, blinds, or window coverings providing privacy
Enclosed room with door visible or implied
negative_indicators: High ambient noise cues (open-plan, public-facing layout)
Bright overhead fluorescent lighting
No horizontal sleeping surface
High foot traffic cues (corridor, lobby adjacency)
Commercial or industrial equipment present
Multiple occupant workstations visible
L059.key_cues_2d: Artifacts/equipment implied by image; layout + furniture topology; signage; surfaces; lighting cues. Key: Bed; dimmable light; privacy; low disturbance cues
L059.vr_enrichment: VR: layout scale; circulation around bed

measurement_items.text: How suitable is this space for sleep (primary)?
How easy would it be to do sleep (primary) here given the visible artifacts/equipment?
How likely would you choose this space for sleep (primary) (all else equal)?
Scenario 2AFC: choose which of two spaces you would use for sleep (primary) (estimate choice probability).
Artifact click/identify: click the object you would primarily use for sleep (primary); RT/accuracy.

L059.vlm_scoring_guidance: Score 1–10 on suitability for sleeping. Look for: (1) presence of a bed as primary artifact, (2) privacy cues (enclosed room, curtains), (3) soft textiles and low-stimulation environment. Penalize for: bright task lighting, public spaces, absence of horizontal rest surface.

L079.name: Cook (Daily)
L079.definition: Suitability of the pictured space for doing cook (daily) (given typical user intentions).
key_spatial_relations: work triangle (stove–sink–refrigerator)
counter adjacency to stove and sink
hard washable surfaces (tile, stone, stainless steel)
storage above/below counters
task lighting over work surfaces
positive_indicators: Utensils, pots, pans, or cooking tools visible
Cutting board or food prep surface
negative_indicators: No cooking appliances present
Absence of hard work surfaces
No water fixture (sink)
Soft furnishings dominate without kitchen fixtures
Dining area only with no cooking zone visible
L079.key_cues_2d: Artifacts/equipment implied by image; layout + furniture topology; signage; surfaces; lighting cues. Key: Stove; sink; counters; prep tools

measurement_items.text: How suitable is this space for cook (daily)?
How easy would it be to do cook (daily) here given the visible artifacts/equipment?
How likely would you choose this space for cook (daily) (all else equal)?
Scenario 2AFC: choose which of two spaces you would use for cook (daily) (estimate choice probability).
Artifact click/identify: click the object you would primarily use for cook (daily); RT/accuracy.

L079.vlm_scoring_guidance: Score 1–10 on suitability for daily cooking. Look for: (1) cooking appliances (stove, oven, microwave), (2) prep surfaces (counters), (3) water access (sink), (4) storage (refrigerator, cabinets). The work triangle layout is a strong positive signal even when individual appliances are partially occluded.

L091.name: Computer Work (Solo)
L091.definition: Suitability of the pictured space for doing computer work (solo) (given typical user intentions).
key_spatial_relations: desk as primary work surface
monitor at eye level
ergonomic seating with back support
task lighting positioned to reduce glare
low visual distraction in immediate field of view
power outlet access at desk level
positive_indicators: Desk or dedicated work surface visible
Monitor, TV screen, or laptop at desk height
Task lamp or directional desk lighting
Door or enclosure providing privacy (for solo work)
negative_indicators: No flat work surface present
Seating not appropriate for extended seated work (e.g., only soft lounging seats, no desk)
High ambient noise or distraction cues (bar, open public space)
Absence of electrical power access cues
Strong recreational/leisure cues (TV wall without desk)
L091.key_cues_2d: Artifacts/equipment implied by image; layout + furniture topology; signage; surfaces; lighting cues. Key: Monitor; desk; outlets; ergonomic chair

measurement_items.text: How suitable is this space for computer work (solo)?
How easy would it be to do computer work (solo) here given the visible artifacts/equipment?
How likely would you choose this space for computer work (solo) (all else equal)?
Scenario 2AFC: choose which of two spaces you would use for computer work (solo) (estimate choice probability).
Artifact click/identify: click the object you would primarily use for computer work (solo); RT/accuracy.

L091.vlm_scoring_guidance: Score 1–10 on suitability for solo computer work. Look for: (1) desk or flat work surface, (2) monitor or laptop, (3) ergonomic seating, (4) task lighting. A hotel room desk scores moderately; a dedicated home office or private office scores highest. Open-plan offices score moderate due to noise/distraction cues.

L130.name: Casual Conversation / Hangout
L130.definition: Suitability of the pictured space for doing casual conversation / hangout (given typical user intentions).
key_spatial_relations: sociopetal seating arrangement (seats facing each other)
soft seating cluster with central focus element (coffee table, rug)
accessible shared surface (coffee table within reach of multiple seats)
comfortable spacing — not too far, not cramped
warm or ambient lighting rather than task lighting
low visual barrier between seats (open sightlines across group)
positive_indicators: Multiple seating pieces arranged facing each other
Coffee table or central shared surface
Rug or soft flooring defining seating zone
TV or focal wall element (optional but common)
Low visual clutter in conversational sight-lines
Social drink/food accessories (wine glasses, cups, snacks)
negative_indicators: Only single-occupancy seating (no seating cluster)
Seating oriented away from each other (parallel rows, classroom)
Strong work task cues overwhelming leisure signals
No soft seating present (only hard chairs at work desks)
High distraction cues (TV screens everywhere without comfortable seating)
L130.key_cues_2d: Artifacts/equipment implied by image; layout + furniture topology; signage; surfaces; lighting cues. Key: Soft seating clusters; coffee table

measurement_items.text: How suitable is this space for casual conversation / hangout?
How easy would it be to do casual conversation / hangout here given the visible artifacts/equipment?
How likely would you choose this space for casual conversation / hangout (all else equal)?
Scenario 2AFC: choose which of two spaces you would use for casual conversation / hangout (estimate choice probability).
Artifact click/identify: click the object you would primarily use for casual conversation / hangout; RT/accuracy.

L130.vlm_scoring_guidance: Score 1–10 on suitability for casual conversation. The key signal is the sociopetal seating arrangement — seats facing each other with a shared central surface. A living room with sofa + coffee table scores high. A bedroom with a sofa area scores moderate. A lone desk chair scores low. Focus on seating cluster topology over individual object presence.

L141.name: Yoga / Stretching
L141.definition: Suitability of the pictured space for doing yoga / stretching (given typical user intentions).
key_spatial_relations: open unobstructed floor space (at least 2m x 1.5m clearance)
full-length or large mirror on wall (for form feedback)
low clutter — no furniture in the movement zone
calm visual field (minimal visual noise)
soft natural or warm lighting
positive_indicators: Yoga mat or exercise mat visible
Large open floor area with no furniture obstruction
Minimal clutter in the floor space
Soft flooring (wood, carpet, rubber) rather than hard concrete
Natural light or warm ambient lighting
Gym or fitness equipment nearby (moderate positive signal)
negative_indicators: Floor fully occupied by furniture (no clearance for mat)
Dense clutter preventing movement
Narrow corridor or restricted space
Industrial equipment cluttering floor
High visual noise / stimulation
L141.key_cues_2d: Artifacts/equipment implied by image; layout + furniture topology; signage; surfaces; lighting cues. Key: Mats; open floor; calm cues

measurement_items.text: How suitable is this space for yoga / stretching?
How easy would it be to do yoga / stretching here given the visible artifacts/equipment?
How likely would you choose this space for yoga / stretching (all else equal)?
Scenario 2AFC: choose which of two spaces you would use for yoga / stretching (estimate choice probability).
Artifact click/identify: click the object you would primarily use for yoga / stretching; RT/accuracy.

L141.vlm_scoring_guidance: Score 1–10 on suitability for yoga/stretching. This affordance is primarily an ABSENCE-OF-CLUTTER signal: a clear floor area is the single strongest predictor. Secondary positives: yoga mat visible, mirror on wall, calm lighting. A large open living room with furniture pushed aside scores higher than a cluttered bedroom even without a mat. Gym spaces with mats score highest.
L141.special_note: L141 is unusual in that its primary positive indicators are architectural/spatial (open floor clearance) rather than object presence. This means COCO segmentation feature vectors will be relatively weak predictors; VLM holistic scene assessment is especially important for this affordance.
